items.name: predictive-demand-forecasting-timeseries
items.description: A structural time-series framework implementing advanced temporal feature engineering and mathematical stationarity transformations to stabilize variance. Trains optimized machine learning forecasting models to project long-term inventory and supply chain demand

items.name: NEGI-Framework
items.description: A machine learning framework for evaluating urban greening energy trade-offs in desalination-dependent cities using the Net Energy Gain Index (NEGI).

items.name: AIP-G-1.0
items.description: The AIP-G 1.0 (Alzheimer’s disease, Inhibitor Predictor for GSK-3β, version 1.0) pipeline implements a two-stage machine learning framework for predicting GSK-3β inhibitors for Alzheimer’s disease.

items.name: EEG-Functional-Connectivity-Graph-Deep-Learning
items.description: Graph-based deep learning framework for EEG functional connectivity analysis and brain state classification (Eyes Open vs Eyes Closed) using machine learning, graph neural networks, and statistical validation

items.name: scalable-inference-serving
items.description: A collection of APIs, frameworks, and platforms for scalable machine learning model inference serving, deployment, and management.

items.name: pytorch

items.name: google-tensorflow

items.name: apache-mahout
items.description: Apache Mahout is an open-source framework for building scalable machine learning applications.

items.name: streamlit
items.description: Streamlit is an open-source Python framework that makes it easy to build and share beautiful, custom web apps for machine learning and data science. With Streamlit, you can turn data scripts into shareable web applications in minutes without needing front-end experience.

items.name: zenml
items.description: ZenML is an open-source MLOps and LLMOps framework that unifies machine learning and generative AI workflows through a single orchestration, versioning, and governance layer.

items.name: amazon-emr
items.description: Amazon EMR is a cloud big data platform for running large-scale distributed data processing jobs, interactive SQL queries, and machine learning applications using open-source analytics frameworks such as Apache Spark, Apache Hive, Apache HBase, Apache Flink, Apache Hudi, and Presto.

items.name: Drug-induced-Autoimmunity-Prediction-Using-Machine-learning
items.description: This project highlights the potential of machine learning in enhancing drug safety evaluations and  provides a reproducible framework for toxicological screening. By leveraging molecular  descriptors and robust classification techniques, the approach contributes to advancements in  computational drug discovery and predictive toxicology.

items.name: Framework-for-Phishing-Website-Detection-Using-URL-Based-Features
items.description: Phishing websites steal sensitive user information by mimicking legitimate websites. This paper proposes a hyperparameter-optimized machine learning framework that improves phishing detection accuracy while reducing false positives using URL and website features.

items.name: Bioactivity-Classification
items.description: A reproducible machine-learning workflow for bioactivity classification using Support Vector Machines (SVM-RBF) and the caret package in R, implementing and extending Max Kuhn’s classification.Rnw framework.

items.name: -Telco-Customer-Churn-Prediction-Comparative-Machine-Learning-Analysis-Dashboard-
items.description: Production-grade ML pipeline & Streamlit dashboard solving subscriber attrition. Built on a decoupled Python framework, it resolves extreme class imbalance using scikit-learn. Benchmarks 6 optimized models (XGBoost, RF, SVM) via a live inference engine to convert variables into real-time risk probabilities & client retention strategies.

items.name: Data_Science

items.name: Real-Time-Multi-Agent-Framework-for-Detecting-Cascading-Failures-in-Machine-Learning-Pipelines
items.description: Real-time ML monitoring system detecting systemic failures across Data, Model, and API layers. Features a multi-agent architecture, synthetic drift engine (Z-score/KL Divergence), and adaptive thresholding to visualize and mitigate cascading risks in machine learning pipelines. Built with FastAPI, NumPy, and Chart.js.

items.name: Credit-Risk-Assessment-Project

items.name: REFLEX
items.description: REFLEX: a machine learning framework for reflexive markets, where the data distribution depends on the model. We solve for a fixed-point equilibrium between a dealer market-making policy and the OTC bond market it shapes.

items.name: mGFD_EcoRisk_Simulator
